sections.paragraph:
ἀπλανής, ές, gen. οῦς adj. (πλάνης ‘wanderer’; since Pla., Aristot., also EpArist 2; Philo; Jos., Ant. 20, 261; Tat. 9, 2; Ath. 6, 3) pert. to not straying from an established or recognized course, unerring, undeviating, correct τὴν ἀπλανῆ θεοσέβειαν faultless worship of God AcPlCor 2:10.—DELG s.v. πλανάομαι.

gen. gen. = genitive

adj. adj. = adjective

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

AcPlCor AcPlCor = Acts of Paul: correspondence with Corinthians (a.k.a. Third Corinthians), text according to PBodmer X—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 104
